# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].
# Category: sql_injection

# app/controllers/admin/reports_controller.rb
class Admin::ReportsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_user!
  before_action :require_admin!
  before_action :set_report, only: [:show, :execute]

  # Predefined, vetted report templates that admins can choose from
  # Each has parameterized placeholders — never raw SQL from users
  ALLOWED_REPORTS = {
    "user_signups_by_period" => {
      description: "User signups grouped by time period",
      sql: <<~SQL,
        SELECT DATE_TRUNC(:period, created_at) AS period_start,
               COUNT(*) AS signup_count
        FROM users
        WHERE created_at BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date
        GROUP BY period_start
        ORDER BY period_start
      SQL
      allowed_params: {
        period: { type: :enum, values: %w[day week month year] },
        start_date: { type: :date },
        end_date: { type: :date }
      }
    },
    "revenue_summary" => {
      description: "Revenue summary by product category",
      sql: <<~SQL,
        SELECT categories.name AS category,
               COUNT(orders.id) AS order_count,
               SUM(orders.total_cents) / 100.0 AS total_revenue
        FROM orders
        INNER JOIN products ON products.id = orders.product_id
        INNER JOIN categories ON categories.id = products.category_id
        WHERE orders.created_at BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date
          AND orders.status = :status
        GROUP BY categories.name
        ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
      SQL
      allowed_params: {
        start_date: { type: :date },
        end_date: { type: :date },
        status: { type: :enum, values: %w[completed refunded pending] }
      }
    },
    "active_users" => {
      description: "Most active users by login count",
      sql: <<~SQL,
        SELECT users.id, users.email, users.name,
               COUNT(login_events.id) AS login_count,
               MAX(login_events.created_at) AS last_login
        FROM users
        INNER JOIN login_events ON login_events.user_id = users.id
        WHERE login_events.created_at >= :since_date
        GROUP BY users.id, users.email, users.name
        ORDER BY login_count DESC
        LIMIT :result_limit
      SQL
      allowed_params: {
        since_date: { type: :date },
        result_limit: { type: :integer, min: 1, max: 500 }
      }
    }
  }.freeze

  MAX_RESULTS = 10_000

  def index
    @reports = ALLOWED_REPORTS.map do |key, config|
      { key: key, description: config[:description], params: config[:allowed_params] }
    end

    render json: { reports: @reports }
  end

  def show
    render json: {
      report_key: @report_key,
      description: @report_config[:description],
      parameters: @report_config[:allowed_params]
    }
  end

  def execute
    validated_params = validate_and_sanitize_report_params!

    results = execute_safe_query(@report_config[:sql], validated_params)

    AuditLog.create!(
      user: current_user,
      action: "report_executed",
      details: { report_key: @report_key, parameters: validated_params }
    )

    render json: {
      report: @report_key,
      parameters: validated_params,
      row_count: results.length,
      columns: results.first&.keys || [],
      rows: results
    }
  rescue ArgumentError, ValidationError => e
    render json: { error: e.message }, status: :unprocessable_entity
  end

  # Admin-only: create new saved report templates that go through review
  def create_template
    template = SavedReportTemplate.new(
      name: params[:name],
      description: params[:description],
      raw_sql: params[:sql],
      parameter_definitions: params[:parameters],
      created_by: current_user,
      status: :pending_review # Requires a second admin to approve
    )

    if template.save
      AdminMailer.report_template_review_requested(template).deliver_later
      render json: {
        message: "Report template submitted for security review",
        template_id: template.id
      }, status: :created
    else
      render json: { errors: template.errors.full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  private

  class ValidationError < StandardError; end

  def set_report
    @report_key = params[:id]
    @report_config = ALLOWED_REPORTS[@report_key]

    unless @report_config
      render json: { error: "Unknown report: #{@report_key}" }, status: :not_found
    end
  end

  def require_admin!
    unless current_user&.admin?
      render json: { error: "Forbidden" }, status: :forbidden
    end
  end

  def validate_and_sanitize_report_params!
    report_params = params[:report_params] || {}
    allowed = @report_config[:allowed_params]
    validated = {}

    allowed.each do |param_name, rules|
      raw_value = report_params[param_name]

      if raw_value.blank?
        raise ValidationError, "Missing required parameter: #{param_name}"
      end

      validated[param_name] = sanitize_param(param_name, raw_value, rules)
    end

    validated
  end

  def sanitize_param(name, value, rules)
    case rules[:type]
    when :date
      date = Date.parse(value.to_s)
      raise ValidationError, "Invalid date for #{name}" if date.year < 2000 || date > Date.tomorrow
      date
    when :integer
      int_val = Integer(value)
      if rules[:min] && int_val < rules[:min]
        raise ValidationError, "#{name} must be >= #{rules[:min]}"
      end
      if rules[:max] && int_val > rules[:max]
        raise ValidationError, "#{name} must be <= #{rules[:max]}"
      end
      int_val
    when :enum
      unless rules[:values].include?(value.to_s)
        raise ValidationError, "#{name} must be one of: #{rules[:values].join(', ')}"
      end
      value.to_s
    else
      raise ValidationError, "Unknown parameter type for #{name}"
    end
  rescue Date::Error, ArgumentError => e
    raise ValidationError, "Invalid value for #{name}: #{e.message}"
  end

  def execute_safe_query(sql_template, validated_params)
    # Use ActiveRecord's built-in sanitization for parameterized queries
    sanitized_binds = validated_params.transform_keys { |k| k.to_s }

    # For enum values that are used in non-bindable positions (like DATE_TRUNC),
    # we've already validated them against a strict allowlist above.
    # Replace enum placeholders directly (safe because values are from allowlist).
    processed_sql = sql_template.dup

    @report_config[:allowed_params].each do |param_name, rules|
      placeholder = ":#{param_name}"
      value = validated_params[param_name]

      if rules[:type] == :enum
        # Already validated against strict allowlist - safe to interpolate
        processed_sql.gsub!(placeholder, ActiveRecord::Base.connection.quote(value))
      end
    end

    # Bind remaining parameters safely using ActiveRecord sanitization
    bind_params = validated_params.reject do |key, _|
      @report_config[:allowed_params][key][:type] == :enum
    end

    # Build sanitized SQL with proper quoting for remaining params
    bind_params.each do |param_name, value|
      placeholder = ":#{param_name}"
      quoted_value = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.quote(value)
      processed_sql.gsub!(placeholder, quoted_value)
    end

    # Execute as READ-ONLY within a transaction to prevent any writes
    results = []
    ActiveRecord::Base.connection.transaction do
      ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute("SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY") rescue nil

      raw_results = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.exec_query(processed_sql)

      if raw_results.length > MAX_RESULTS
        raise ValidationError, "Result set too large (#{raw_results.length} rows). Max: #{MAX_RESULTS}"
      end

      results = raw_results.to_a

      raise ActiveRecord::Rollback # Always rollback — this is read-only
    end

    results
  end
end
